title: Capital intensive and labour intensive operations
summary: 9609 AS — choosing between automation and labour in production.
learningObjectives: Define capital and labour intensive operations.

simpleExplanation.title: Machines vs people
simpleExplanation.summary: Capital-intensive firms invest heavily in equipment; labour-intensive firms rely more on workforce skills and hours.
steps: Capital intensive: high fixed costs, low unit cost at scale.
Labour intensive: flexible, lower initial investment.
Wage rates and technology affect choice.

sections.content: **Capital vs labour intensity** (4.1.3) links to **4.1.4 methods** and **finance** later. Judge **fit for market size**.
